canonical_definition: A training-free, zero-shot evaluation framework where a model must determine if a test item X is representationally closer to item A or item B, used to measure the structure of embedding spaces.
papers: Video2Roleplay: A Multimodal Dataset and Framework for Video-Guided Role-playing Agents

definitions.text: A training-free, zero-shot evaluation framework where a model must determine if a test item X is representationally closer to item A or item B, used to measure the structure of embedding spaces.
definitions.paper: Video2Roleplay: A Multimodal Dataset and Framework for Video-Guided Role-playing Agents

definitions.text: An evaluation procedure where a model is presented with three stimuli (A, B, X) and must decide whether X is more similar to A or B, often used with minimal pairs to probe specific linguistic distinctions.

characterization: A training-free, zero-shot evaluation framework where a model must determine if a test item X is representationally closer to item A or item B, used to measure the structure of embedding spaces.

emnlp25: Video2Roleplay: A Multimodal Dataset and Framework for Video-Guided Role-playing Agents
